messages.content: Hey guys! Random question—what were your all-time favorite cartoons growing up? I was just reminiscing and got super nostalgic 😂
Oh man, so many classics! I was obsessed with Dexter’s Laboratory and Ed, Edd n Eddy. 😂 Also, Saturday mornings weren’t complete without some Looney Tunes. What about you, Alice?

Man, Saturday mornings were the best! I’d legit get up early just to pour myself a giant bowl of cereal and watch cartoons for hours. SpongeBob was my go-to, but I also loved Pokémon and Batman: The Animated Series. Total nostalgia trip!

Haha yes, the cereal-and-cartoons ritual! SpongeBob was legendary, I still quote it way too much. 😂 I remember being super into Pokémon too—had the cards, the games, everything. Batman: The Animated Series was so good, way darker than I realized as a kid. Did either of you ever watch Courage the Cowardly Dog? That show lowkey creeped me out but I couldn’t stop watching!

Dude, Courage was wild 😂 Some of those episodes were legit nightmare fuel, but I couldn’t look away either. That show had some seriously weird vibes. Anyone else remember The Grim Adventures of Billy & Mandy? That one cracked me up, especially Grim’s accent. Man, cartoons back then just hit different!

Omg yes, Billy & Mandy! Grim’s accent was iconic 😂 I loved how weird and dark that show was but still hilarious. Did anyone else watch Powerpuff Girls? I was obsessed with them, especially Bubbles. Also, Fairly OddParents was a huge fave—I wanted my own fairy godparents so bad!

Powerpuff Girls was awesome! Mojo Jojo was the funniest villain 😆 And Fairly OddParents had some wild episodes—Cosmo was just pure chaos. Did either of you watch Kim Possible? I thought she was the coolest, and the theme song still pops into my head sometimes. It’s crazy how many good cartoons there were!

Oh man, Kim Possible! “Call me, beep me, if you wanna reach me…” That theme song is still a banger 😂 I totally wanted to be a secret agent after watching that. Anyone remember Recess? I feel like that show nailed what it was like to be a kid at school. And don’t even get me started on Avatar: The Last Airbender—still holds up so well!

Recess was such a vibe! The playground politics felt so real 😂 And Avatar is an all-timer, honestly—such good storytelling and the characters were awesome. I still rewatch it every couple years. Did anyone else used to watch Foster’s Home for Imaginary Friends? That show was so creative, and I loved Cheese—even if he was a total weirdo!

Yes! Foster’s was SO good—Cheese cracked me up every time with his “I like chocolate milk” 😂 That show had such a cool art style too. I feel like Cartoon Network had so many gems back then. Did you guys ever watch Codename: Kids Next Door? I always wanted to be part of their treehouse squad!

Oh, absolutely! KND was awesome—I always thought their gadgets made out of random stuff were so cool. Their treehouse was basically every kid’s dream headquarters 😂 Number One was my favorite, but I also wanted to be as chill as Number Five. Did you guys ever get into Rocket Power or Hey Arnold? Those shows made me want to skateboard and live in a big city so bad!

Rocket Power! “Woogity woogity woogity!” 😂 That show made me think surfing and skating were the coolest things ever, even though I was definitely not coordinated enough. Hey Arnold was so good too—the city vibes, the jazz music, and honestly, Helga was hilarious. Anyone remember As Told by Ginger? That one hit me right in the feels sometimes!

As Told by Ginger! That show got way deeper than I expected for a cartoon, honestly. The theme song still hits me with nostalgia. I remember thinking Dodie was hilarious with all her gossip 😂 Did you guys ever watch Ed, Edd n Eddy? That show was just straight-up chaos, but it always made me laugh. The jawbreakers were bigger than their heads!

Ed, Edd n Eddy was pure chaos, I swear 😂 The scams, the sound effects, and those jawbreakers… iconic! Sometimes I still say “dork” in Double D’s voice. Did you ever watch Dexter’s Laboratory? Dee Dee always getting Dexter in trouble cracked me up every time. I also loved The Wild Thornberrys—Eliza talking to animals was so cool to me!

Dexter’s Lab was so good! I always wanted my own secret lab, but my room was just full of random junk 😅 Dee Dee was such an agent of chaos. The Wild Thornberrys was a classic too—Nigel’s voice still cracks me up to this day (“Smashing!”). Did anyone else watch Rugrats? Tommy and the crew seriously made everyday stuff into wild adventures. I lowkey wanted a Reptar bar so bad!

Rugrats was legendary! I swear every time they went on one of their little adventures, I wanted to crawl around the house pretending I was on a mission too 😂 And honestly, Reptar merch still looks awesome. Anyone else remember Doug? That theme song is stuck in my head forever, and I always thought Quailman was the funniest superhero. There were just so many good shows back then!

Omg yes, Doug! The “do do do do do do…” theme song is instantly in my head now 😂 Quailman was iconic with his underwear over his shorts—I definitely tried the look once as a kid (no regrets). Did either of you ever watch Arthur? DW was such a mood, and that whole show was just super comforting after school. So many memes came from it too!

Arthur! That show was such a staple after school for me. DW was savage before I even knew what that meant 😂 And the “having fun isn’t hard when you’ve got a library card” song still pops in my head sometimes. I feel like PBS had some gems—Cyberchase and Dragon Tales too! Anyone else secretly wish they could jump into a computer or hang with dragons?

Dragon Tales! I totally forgot about that one—those dragon stones were so cool, I wanted one so bad. And Cyberchase was legit, the whole “save cyberspace” thing made math look way more fun than school ever did 😂 Do you guys remember Zoom? Not a cartoon, but that show had me trying all their weird experiments and games at home. Childhood TV was undefeated, honestly.

Zoom! Oh man, “send it to Zoom!” I always wanted to try out their crafts but somehow my version never looked like theirs 😂 Childhood TV really was unbeatable—Saturday mornings in pajamas, cereal in hand, it was the best. Did either of you ever get into Animaniacs or Pinky and the Brain? The jokes went over my head back then, but I appreciate them so much more now!
Animaniacs was wild, haha. I definitely didn’t get half the jokes as a kid, but Yakko’s “Nations of the World” song is still impressive.

needle_message: Man, Saturday mornings were the best! I’d legit get up early just to pour myself a giant bowl of cereal and watch cartoons for hours. SpongeBob was my go-to, but I also loved Pokémon and Batman: The Animated Series. Total nostalgia trip!
